mappings.question: Aisha drinks 1/4 of a bottle of water before lunch and another 1/4 after lunch. What fraction of the bottle has she drunk altogether?
mappings.explanation: Aisha drinks 1/4 + 1/4 = 2/4. Simplify: 2/4 = 1/2 of the bottle. ✓

mappings.question: Which fraction is equivalent to 3/6?
mappings.explanation: Simplify 3/6 by dividing both numbers by 3: 3÷3 = 1 and 6÷3 = 2, so 3/6 = 1/2. ✓

mappings.question: There are 20 children in a swimming class. 3/4 of them can swim a full length. How many children can swim a full length?
Which of these fractions is the largest? 2/5, 3/10, 1/2, 3/5, 4/10
A bowl is 5/8 full of flour. Jack uses 1/8 of the bowl for a recipe. What fraction of the bowl is left?
mappings.explanation: Jack has 5/8 and uses 1/8: 5/8 - 1/8 = 4/8. Simplify: 4/8 = 1/2 of the bowl left. ✓

mappings.question: In a class of 16 pupils, 12 bought their lunch from the canteen. What fraction bought their lunch? Give your answer in its simplest form.
A football club has 18 members. 2/3 of them turn up for Saturday practice. How many members turn up?
Which fraction is equivalent to 0.25?
Ella fills 3/5 of her water bottle at home and adds another 1/5 at school. What fraction of the bottle is full now?
mappings.explanation: Add the numerators: 3/5 + 1/5 = 4/5 of the bottle is full. ✓

mappings.question: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Tom eats 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
Two jugs are the same size. One is 2/3 full of squash and the other is 3/4 full. Which jug has more squash?
mappings.explanation: Convert to twelfths: 2/3 = 8/12 and 3/4 = 9/12. Since 9/12 > 8/12, then 3/4 is larger. ✓

mappings.question: Lily eats 1/2 of a cake on Saturday and 1/4 on Sunday. What fraction of the cake has she eaten altogether?
mappings.explanation: Convert to quarters: 1/2 = 2/4. Then 2/4 + 1/4 = 3/4 of the cake eaten. ✓

mappings.question: In a pack of 24 coloured pencils, 18 are sharp. What fraction of the pencils are sharp? Give your answer in its simplest form.
Noah has 8 metres of ribbon. He uses 3/4 of it for a craft project. How many metres does he use?
Sarah has 35 paperback books and 20 hardback books. What fraction of her books are hardbacks?
mappings.explanation: Total books = 35 + 20 = 55. Hardbacks = 20/55. Simplify: 20÷5 = 4 and 55÷5 = 11, so 4/11. ✓

mappings.question: Which fraction is equivalent to 4/8?
A baker makes 30 biscuits. He gives 2/5 of them to his neighbours. How many biscuits does he give away?
In a bag of 60 sweets, 15 are red. What fraction are red?
What is 5/6 + 1/6?
Which of these fractions is closest to 1? 7/8, 5/6, 9/10, 4/5, 11/12
mappings.explanation: Distance from 1: 7/8 = 1/8 away, 5/6 = 1/6 away, 9/10 = 1/10 away, 4/5 = 1/5 away, 11/12 = 1/12 away. Smallest distance is 1/12, so 11/12 is closest. ✓

mappings.question: What is 3/4 - 1/4?
Convert 0.6 to a fraction in its simplest form.
A chocolate bar has 24 pieces. Emily eats 1/3 of it. How many pieces does she eat?
Harry walks 2/3 of a mile to school and then 1/6 of a mile to the park. What fraction of a mile has he walked altogether?
mappings.explanation: Convert to sixths: 2/3 = 4/6. Then 4/6 + 1/6 = 5/6 of a mile in total. ✓

mappings.question: In a class of 30 pupils, 20 have a school dinner. What fraction have a school dinner? Give your answer in its simplest form.
A school choir has 45 members. 4/5 of them attend a concert. How many members attend?
mappings.explanation: Find 1/5 of 45 first: 45 ÷ 5 = 9. Then 4/5 = 4 × 9 = 36 members attend. ✓

mappings.question: Which fraction equals 75%?
A jug is 7/8 full of orange juice. Dad pours out 3/8 of the jug. What fraction of the jug still has juice?
mappings.explanation: The jug had 7/8 and Dad poured out 3/8: 7/8 - 3/8 = 4/8. Simplify: 4/8 = 1/2 of the jug left. ✓

mappings.question: In a test with 50 questions, Jake answered 40 correctly. What fraction did he get correct?
In a garden, 1/3 is planted with vegetables and 1/6 with herbs. What fraction of the garden is planted altogether?
mappings.explanation: Convert to sixths: 1/3 = 2/6. Then 2/6 + 1/6 = 3/6. Simplify: 3/6 = 1/2 of the garden is planted. ✓

mappings.question: Which is the smallest fraction? 1/3, 2/5, 3/8, 1/2, 3/10
There are 12 eggs in a box. Mum uses 5/6 of them to make a cake. How many eggs does she use?
A ribbon is 3/4 metre long. How many centimetres is this?
Ravi eats 1/3 of a pie at lunch and another 1/3 after school. What fraction of the pie has he eaten?
A quiz asks: which fraction is equivalent to 4/10?
There are 35 children in a year group. 2/5 of them sign up for the school play. How many children sign up?
What is 7/9 - 2/9?
In a baking contest, 15 out of 25 cakes are chocolate. What is 15/25 in its simplest form?
A craft box has 24 coloured buttons. Priya uses 3/8 of them for her project. How many buttons does she use?
What is 1/4 + 3/4?
On a spelling test, Amara gets 16 out of 20 correct. What is 16/20 in its simplest form?
Which is larger: 3/5 or 5/8?
mappings.explanation: Convert to 40ths: 3/5 = 24/40 and 5/8 = 25/40. Since 25/40 > 24/40, then 5/8 is larger. ✓

mappings.question: Maya paints 2/3 of a fence in the morning and 1/12 more after lunch. What fraction of the fence has she painted?
A cake is cut into 12 equal pieces. Lucy eats 5 pieces. What fraction is left?
A tin of paint is 5/6 full. Ruby uses 1/6 of the tin for her art project. What fraction of the tin is left?
mappings.explanation: The tin had 5/6 and Ruby used 1/6: 5/6 - 1/6 = 4/6. Simplify: 4/6 = 2/3 of the tin left. ✓

mappings.question: Which fraction is equivalent to 0.75?
What is 3/7 of 28?
What is 2/9 + 5/9?
At a school sports day, 14 out of 21 events are running races. What is 14/21 in its simplest form?
What is 4/9 of 27?
Emma has 60 stickers. She gives away 3/5 of them. How many does she give away?
Which fraction is equivalent to 0.6?
Jake drinks 1/6 of a carton of juice before school and 1/3 at break time. What fraction has he drunk altogether?
Simplify 20/28.
There are 32 children going on a school trip. 7/8 of them bring a packed lunch. How many children bring a packed lunch?
mappings.explanation: 7/8 of 32 = (7 × 32) ÷ 8 = 224 ÷ 8 = 28 children bring a packed lunch. ✓

mappings.question: Tom has read 5/8 of a 160-page book. How many pages has he read?
What is 4/7 + 2/7?
Which is larger: 2/5 or 3/7?
mappings.explanation: Convert to 35ths: 2/5 = 14/35 and 3/7 = 15/35. Since 15/35 > 14/35, then 3/7 is larger. ✓

mappings.question: A roll of sticky tape is 8/12 full. After wrapping a present, 2/12 has been used. What fraction is left?
Simplify 18/27.
There are 48 crayons in a box. Leah uses 5/12 of them for her drawing. How many crayons does she use?
A recipe needs 2/3 cup of sugar. Sophie has 1/6 cup. How much more does she need?
mappings.explanation: Convert to sixths: 2/3 = 4/6. She needs 4/6 - 1/6 = 3/6. Simplify: 3/6 = 1/2 cup. ✓

mappings.question: Which fraction equals 25%?
Simplify 35/49.
On sports day, 20 races are planned. 3/4 of them are completed before it rains. How many races are completed?
Ben travels 3/8 of a 64 km journey. How far has he travelled?
What is 5/9 + 3/9?
Which is the smallest: 1/3, 2/7, 3/10, or 1/4?
Simplify 28/35.
A netball club has 45 members. 2/5 of them are in Year 5. How many members are in Year 5?
What is 1/8 + 3/8?
A garden is 3/4 acre. What decimal is this?
Which fraction is equivalent to 0.5?
Freya walks 1/5 of a mile to the shops and 3/10 of a mile back a different way. What fraction of a mile does she walk in total?
Simplify 22/33.
A PE teacher has 33 tennis balls. She puts 4/11 of them into a basket. How many balls are in the basket?
Mia drinks 2/7 of a 700 ml bottle. How much does she drink?
Simplify 24/32.
What is 5/8 × 16?
In a class of 32 students, 24 passed a test. What fraction passed?
What is 1/5 + 2/5?
Which is the smallest fraction? 1/4, 2/7, 3/10, 1/3, 2/9
What is 4/5 - 1/5?
Convert 0.4 to a fraction in its simplest form.
What is 2/7 + 3/7?
A jug holds 2 litres. How much is 3/4 of the jug in millilitres?
What is 5/9 + 2/9?
Which is closer to 1/2: 4/9 or 5/11?
Simplify 27/36.
A recipe makes 50 biscuits. Oscar gives 7/10 of them to his friends. How many biscuits does he give away?
What is 3/8 + 1/8?
Which fraction equals 60%?
What is 8/9 - 2/9?
In a bag of 45 marbles, 18 are blue. What fraction are blue?
Grace eats 1/4 of a chocolate bar after school and 1/8 after dinner. What fraction of the bar has she eaten in total?
mappings.explanation: Convert to eighths: 1/4 = 2/8. Then 2/8 + 1/8 = 3/8 of the bar eaten. ✓

mappings.question: Simplify 30/45.
What is 4/7 of 42?
Which is the largest fraction? 5/6, 7/8, 4/5, 9/10, 11/12
Oliver has read 5/6 of his book. His sister has read 1/3 of the same book. How much more of the book has Oliver read than his sister?
mappings.explanation: Convert to sixths: 1/3 = 2/6. Then 5/6 - 2/6 = 3/6. Simplify: 3/6 = 1/2 more of the book. ✓

mappings.question: Convert 0.8 to a fraction in its simplest form.
A box of 56 chocolates has 21 dark chocolates. What fraction are dark?
What is 1/2 + 1/5?
Simplify 36/48.
What is 5/8 × 24?
A rope is 5/8 metre long. How many centimetres is this?
Which fraction equals 40%?
Zara fills 3/4 of her water bottle in the morning and adds another 1/8 at lunchtime. What fraction of the bottle is now full?
mappings.explanation: Convert to eighths: 3/4 = 6/8. Then 6/8 + 1/8 = 7/8 of the bottle is full. ✓

mappings.question: Simplify 42/56.
What is 5/9 of 36?
What is 4/9 + 2/9?
In a test with 80 questions, Emma answered 64 correctly. What fraction did she get correct?
What is 1/3 + 1/9?
Which is smaller: 4/7 or 5/9?
mappings.explanation: Convert to 63rds: 4/7 = 36/63 and 5/9 = 35/63. Since 35/63 < 36/63, then 5/9 is smaller. ✓

mappings.question: Simplify 45/60.
What is 7/12 × 24?
What is 5/7 + 1/7?
A bag contains 72 sweets. If 2/9 are strawberry flavour, how many are strawberry?
What is 3/5 - 1/5?
Convert 0.35 to a fraction in its simplest form.
Which fraction is closest to 1? 8/9, 6/7, 10/11, 5/6, 12/13
mappings.explanation: Distance from 1: 8/9 = 1/9 away, 6/7 = 1/7 away, 10/11 = 1/11 away, 5/6 = 1/6 away, 12/13 = 1/13 away. Smallest distance is 1/13, so 12/13 is closest. ✓

mappings.question: After eating 3/5 of his sweets, Tom has 24 left. How many sweets did he start with?
mappings.explanation: If Tom ate 3/5, he has 2/5 left. 2/5 of the total = 24 sweets. So 1/5 = 24 \u00f7 2 = 12 sweets. Total = 5 \u00d7 12 = 60 sweets. \u2713

mappings.question: A recipe uses 2/3 of a bag of flour. Each bag weighs 1.5 kg. How many grams of flour are used?
Lily has 3/4 of a pizza. She gives 2/3 of what she has to her brother. What fraction of the whole pizza does her brother get?
mappings.explanation: Brother gets 2/3 of 3/4 = 2/3 \u00d7 3/4 = 6/12 = 1/2 of the whole pizza. \u2713

mappings.question: Sam walks 1/4 of a mile to school, 1/3 of a mile to the shops, and 5/12 of a mile home. How far does he walk in total?
In a class, 1/4 of the pupils walk to school and 30% cycle. What fraction of the class either walk or cycle?
A builder has 3 1/2 metres of wood. He uses 1 3/4 metres for a shelf. How much wood is left?
Oliver has \u00a348. He spends 3/8 on books and 1/6 on a gift. How much does he have left?
A bag has 15 red, 10 blue, and 5 green marbles. What fraction of the marbles are NOT blue?
mappings.explanation: Total marbles = 15 + 10 + 5 = 30. Not blue = 15 + 5 = 20. Fraction = 20/30. Simplify: 20 \u00f7 10 = 2 and 30 \u00f7 10 = 3, so 2/3. \u2713

mappings.question: Which of these has the greatest value? 3/8, 0.45, 37%, 2/5, 7/16
Priya spends 1/4 of her money on a book and 1/3 on a game. She has \u00a325 left. How much money did she start with?
What is 3/5 \u00d7 5/9?
Three children share a 2.4 kg bag of oranges. Ava takes 1/3, Ben takes 3/8 of what is left, and Charlie gets the rest. How many grams does Charlie get?
mappings.explanation: 2.4 kg = 2400 g. Ava takes 1/3 of 2400 = 800 g. Remaining = 1600 g. Ben takes 3/8 of 1600 = 600 g. Charlie gets 1600 - 600 = 1000 g. \u2713

mappings.question: A water tank is 5/6 full. Then 3/8 of the tank is drained. What fraction of the tank is still full?
Hannah runs 2 1/3 km on Monday and 1 5/6 km on Tuesday. How far does she run in total?
A school has 240 pupils. 3/8 are in Key Stage 1 and 5/12 are in lower Key Stage 2. The rest are in upper Key Stage 2. How many pupils are in upper Key Stage 2?
